title: Function App Sample (Ingestion API)
descriptionMarkdown: The **Function App Sample** data connector demonstrates how to ingest custom JSON events into a Microsoft Sentinel custom table using an Azure Function and the [Azure Monitor Ingestion API](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/azure-monitor/logs/logs-ingestion-api-overview) (DCE/DCR). Use this as a starting template for building your own Azure Function–based connectors.

sampleQueries.query:
FunctionAppSample_CL
| where EventType == "Alert"
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

dataTypes.name: FunctionAppSample_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
FunctionAppSample_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

value:
FunctionAppSample_CL
            | summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
            | project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(1d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

customs.name: Microsoft.Web/sites permissions
customs.description: Read and write permissions to Azure Functions to create a Function App is required. [See the documentation to learn more about Azure Functions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/azure-functions/).

customs.name: Azure AD App Registration
customs.description: An Azure AD App Registration (service principal) is required. The ARM template will automatically assign the **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role on the Data Collection Rule (DCR) to the App Registration using its Object ID. [Learn more about registering an application](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-register-app).

instructionSteps.description: >**NOTE:** This connector uses Azure Functions and the Azure Monitor Ingestion API (DCE/DCR) to push sample events into Microsoft Sentinel. The ARM template automatically creates the Data Collection Endpoint, custom log table (`FunctionAppSample_CL`), Data Collection Rule, and role assignment — no manual Azure Monitor configuration is required. This might result in additional data ingestion costs. Check the [Azure Functions pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/functions/) and [Azure Monitor pricing page](https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/monitor/) for details.

>**(Optional Step)** Securely store your App Registration credentials in Azure Key Vault. [Follow these instructions](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/app-service-key-vault-references) to use Azure Key Vault references with an Azure Function App.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 1 – Create an Azure AD App Registration
instructionSteps.description:
1. In the [Azure Portal](https://portal.azure.com), navigate to **Azure Active Directory > App registrations > New registration**.
2. Provide a name (e.g. `FunctionApp-Sentinel-Connector`) and register.
3. Under **Certificates & secrets**, create a new client secret. Note the **Tenant ID**, **Client ID**, and **Client Secret**.
4. On the **Overview** page, also note the **Object ID** — this is required by the ARM template to assign the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the Data Collection Rule.

instructionSteps.title: STEP 2 – Deploy the Azure Function App
instructionSteps.description:
Click **Deploy to Azure** and fill in the parameters. The template will automatically create the Data Collection Endpoint, `FunctionAppSample_CL` table, Data Collection Rule, role assignment, and Function App.

[![Deploy To Azure](https://aka.ms/deploytoazurebutton)](https://portal.azure.com/#create/Microsoft.Template/uri/https%3A%2F%2Fraw.githubusercontent.com%2Frobertmoriarty12%2FAzure-Sentinel%2Fmaster%2FSolutions%2FFunctionApp%2FData%2520Connectors%2Fazuredeploy_FunctionApp_API_FunctionApp.json)

**Parameters to fill in:**

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `WorkspaceName` | Name of your Log Analytics / Sentinel workspace |
| `TenantId` | Azure AD Tenant ID from Step 1 |
| `ClientId` | App Registration Client ID from Step 1 |
| `ClientSecret` | App Registration Client Secret from Step 1 |
| `AzureClientObjectId` | App Registration **Object ID** from Step 1 |
| `AppInsightsWorkspaceResourceID` | Full Resource ID of the Log Analytics workspace (from **Log Analytics workspace > Properties**) |
